codeReview.summary: The scaffold cleanly establishes a local-only Compose shell with top-level navigation, lightweight application/container wiring, placeholder feature entry points, and baseline JVM plus instrumentation smoke coverage. I did not find any high-confidence correctness or mission-boundary issues in this feature.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared architecture docs still list the suggested module shape without the new `core:designsystem` module, even though this feature established it as the shared home for app-shell and placeholder UI primitives. Future workers may miss that reusable screen scaffolds and theming now live there.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md:102-115 lists modules but omits `core:designsystem`; compare to settings.gradle.kts:19-31 including `:core:designsystem` and core/designsystem/build.gradle.kts:1-43 defining the module.

summary: Pass. The app shell scaffold matches the feature contract: it launches into a stable local-only Compose shell with navigation entry points for hosts, identities, sessions, snippets, and settings; lightweight DI/application wiring is in place; and baseline unit plus connected Android smoke tests cover the new shell.
